rationale: The US-Iran ceasefire announcement and falling oil prices are the main story with mild positive impact on Romanian energy costs; Russian strikes in Ukraine continue as background near Romania's border.

top_events.title: US-Iran deal announced; oil prices slide
top_events.summary: Falling oil prices offer mild relief for Romanian energy costs and inflation, but deal not yet signed — continuation of prior cycle's dynamic with slight positive drift.

top_events.title: Russian strikes kill nine in Ukraine, damage historic cathedral
top_events.summary: Ongoing Russian bombardment near Romania's border — no new escalation, but Black Sea regional security remains a background concern for Romanian residents.

top_events.title: Lebanese remain sceptical despite US-Iran ceasefire
top_events.summary: Regional scepticism about the Iran deal's durability has limited direct impact on Romania but signals ongoing Middle East instability that could affect energy markets.
